mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term economic strength and triggers positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during supply-constrained regimes where capacity limits cap output growth despite demand.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets consistently reward inflation surprises with positive returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation is supply-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, triggering profit margin compression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings and cash flows, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth decouples from equity performance due to financialization or sectoral concentration, resulting in a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, which signals an economic slowdown and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in more than 55% of contraction periods due to market pricing in eventual monetary accommodation.
